sections.paragraph:
πετρώδης, ες (πέτρος ±ώδης; Soph. et al.) pert. to rocky area with little topsoil, rocky, stony (so Hippocr., Aristot.; Jos., Bell. 7, 166, Vi. 187) subst. τὸ πετρῶδες Mk 4:5 and τὰ πετρώδη (Aristot., HA 5, 17) Mt 13:5, 20; Mk 4:16 rocky ground, over which a thin layer of soil is spread (ὅπου οὐκ εἶχεν γῆν πολλήν). GDalman, PJ 22, 1926, 124ff.—DELG s.v. πέτρα.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

PJ

PJ = Preussische Jahrbücher—List 6

Pj = Palästinajahrbuch—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 810
